root_text.an5.155:1.1: “Pañcime, bhikkhave, dhammā saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.an5.155:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhū dhammaṁ na pariyāpuṇanti—
root_text.an5.155:1.4: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an5.155:1.5: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo dhammo saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ na vitthārena paresaṁ desenti.
root_text.an5.155:2.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo dhammo saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ na vitthārena paraṁ vācenti.
root_text.an5.155:3.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo dhammo saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ na vitthārena sajjhāyaṁ karonti.
root_text.an5.155:4.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho dhammo saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ na cetasā anuvitakkenti anuvicārenti manasānupekkhanti.
root_text.an5.155:5.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamo dhammo saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:5.3: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca dhammā saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.an5.155:6.1: Pañcime, bhikkhave, dhammā saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.an5.155:6.4: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an5.155:6.5: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo dhammo saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ vitthārena paresaṁ desenti.
root_text.an5.155:7.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo dhammo saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ vitthārena paraṁ vācenti.
root_text.an5.155:8.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo dhammo saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ vitthārena sajjhāyaṁ karonti.
root_text.an5.155:9.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho dhammo saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhū yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ cetasā anuvitakkenti anuvicārenti manasānupekkhanti.
root_text.an5.155:10.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamo dhammo saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.155:10.3: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca dhammā saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattantī”ti.

translation_text.an5.155:0.3: The Decline of the True Teaching (2nd)
translation_text.an5.155:1.1: “Mendicants, these five things lead to the decline and disappearance of the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.155:1.3: It’s when the mendicants don’t memorize the teaching—
translation_text.an5.155:1.4: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an5.155:1.5: This is the first thing that leads to the decline and disappearance of the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.155:2.1: Furthermore, the mendicants don’t explain the teaching in detail to others as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:3.1: Furthermore, the mendicants don’t make others recite the teaching in detail as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:4.1: Furthermore, the mendicants don’t rehearse the teaching in detail as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:5.1: Furthermore, the mendicants don’t think about and consider the teaching in their hearts, examining it with their minds as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:5.2: This is the fifth thing that leads to the decline and disappearance of the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.155:5.3: These five things lead to the decline and disappearance of the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.155:6.1: These five things lead to the continuation, persistence, and enduring of the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.155:6.3: It’s when the mendicants memorize the teaching—
translation_text.an5.155:6.4: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an5.155:6.5: This is the first thing that leads to the continuation, persistence, and enduring of the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.155:7.1: Furthermore, the mendicants explain the teaching in detail to others as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:8.1: Furthermore, the mendicants make others recite the teaching in detail as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:9.1: Furthermore, the mendicants rehearse the teaching in detail as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:10.1: Furthermore, the mendicants think about and consider the teaching in their hearts, examining it with their minds as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.155:10.2: This is the fifth thing that leads to the continuation, persistence, and enduring of the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.155:10.3: These five things lead to the continuation, persistence, and enduring of the true teaching.”

variant_text.an5.155:3.1: paraṁ → paresaṁ (bj, cck, km, pts1ed); pare (?)
